projectDescription: A self-hosted WebRTC monitoring system that collects, ingests, and visualizes real-time call metrics to help developers troubleshoot issues and optimize performance.
keyFeatures: App-level dashboard with browser, OS, and location breakdowns
Automatic detection of WebRTC connection issues
Detailed per-participant reports with device info and timeline
Drilldown connection timelines for the full duration of a call
Self-hosted: full control over data and infrastructure
Separate scalable API (ingestion) and Web (visualization) services
JavaScript SDK integration with Livekit, Mediasoup, Janus, and custom WebRTC
Docker Compose and Kubernetes deployment support
POST_CONFERENCE_CLEANUP flag to manage storage efficiently
publicApiSurface: POST /v1 — metrics ingestion endpoint (used by JS SDK)
PeerMetrics({ apiRoot, apiKey, userId, userName, conferenceId, conferenceName, appVersion }) — JS SDK constructor
DEFAULT_ADMIN_USERNAME env var — customize admin username
DEFAULT_ADMIN_PASSWORD env var — customize admin password (api + web)
DEFAULT_ADMIN_EMAIL env var — customize admin email
INIT_TOKEN_SECRET env var — JWT token secret
SESSION_TOKEN_SECRET env var — session cookie encryption key
API_ROOT env var — URL of the API service for the web service
WEB_DOMAIN env var — domain of the web service
REDIS_HOST env var — Redis connection URL
POST_CONFERENCE_CLEANUP env var — delete stats events after conference ends
URL_PREFIX env var — subpath prefix for serving under a non-root path
docker compose up — start all services
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yaml up — start in development mode
kubectl apply -k k8s/overlays/<env> — deploy to Kubernetes
